canonicalSpecs.purpose: Repository root directory dictionary and workspace metadata.
Cross-language code organization and focused file boundaries.
Canonical domain, capability, package, and component naming.
Rust crate/module layout, errors, async, and verification.
TypeScript package structure and typed SDK ports.
Drive spaces, nodes, upload, download, and storage lifecycle.
HTTP API and OpenAPI contract standard.
Mandatory sdkwork-web-framework integration for HTTP route crates.
Primary SDK standard and generated client integration.
Database contract, table naming, and schema evolution.
Application database lifecycle and SPI orchestration.
Tenants, users, sessions, and dual-token claims.
Token model, authn/authz, and tenant isolation.
Contract tests, module tests, and parity tests.
Uniform public command surfaces for pnpm scripts.
GitHub Actions packaging and release workflow standard.

integration.authority: Root SDKWork specs remain authoritative. Local specs may extend but must not contradict them.
integration.dependencyPolicy: Consumers integrate Drive through generated SDK families, Drive Uploader facades, or declared service ports. Consumers must not copy Drive routes, upload DTOs, storage schemas, or permission catalogs.
integration.sdkPolicy: Generated Drive SDK clients are injected through service/runtime boundaries; frontend packages must not use raw Drive HTTP or manual auth headers.
integration.languagePolicy: Language-specific packages follow native manifests while preserving the same Drive component contract.
integration.webFramework: Drive HTTP route crates integrate through sdkwork-web-framework.
integration.database: Drive database lifecycle is managed by sdkwork-database contracts and migrations.
integration.utils: Drive TypeScript and Rust code use @sdkwork/utils and sdkwork-utils-rust for shared helpers.
